title: Code Review verdict: APPROVE — ADR-0002 @ cde772a (3 pure utils, 2 non-gating low notes)
description: Reviewed slug.ts/num.ts/str.ts against the live tree at commit cde772a (hash matched handoff, clean tree). 24/24 green, tsc clean, zero cross-imports, zero call sites — all verified independently. No security or correctness defects. Verdict APPROVE. Two low-severity notes are forward-looking (Unicode handling) for when these utils get wired to live data, not merge blockers. Accept/reject each so they stay tracked and aren't re-flagged.

title: Security re-scan — 2 Medium findings STILL OPEN (local-seo-agency @ cde772a) — 6th scan
description: Re-scan at cde772a confirms both prior Medium findings remain unremediated (no overrides block in package.json — verified overrides:null; page.tsx:66-67 markup unchanged). 0 new security issues. ADR-0002 modules pure/clean. @google/generative-ai still unused. This is the 6th consecutive identical scan — consider consolidating into a standing ticket.

title: Release v0.2.6 created locally — approve to publish + resolve recurring orphaned-tag drift
description: A PATCH release (0.2.0→0.2.6) was committed and tagged locally (commit ca0617d, annotated tag v0.2.6). It adds three pure-utility lib modules (slug/num/str), a node --test harness (24 tests passing), and ADR/planning docs. Versioning skipped 0.2.1–0.2.5 because those tags persist on commits orphaned by six workspace resets. Decide whether to publish and how to resolve the recurring orphaned-tag drift.
